name: Document Intelligence
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure Document Intelligence
services.purpose: OCR extraction, layout analysis, key-value pairs, table detection, custom models

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for document summarization, entity extraction, and classification

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Document intake — PDFs, images, scanned documents, processed output storage

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Document processing pipeline — intake, OCR, enrichment, output orchestration

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Extracted document metadata, entity store, classification results

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys, storage connection strings, Document Intelligence credentials

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Processing pipeline tracing, extraction accuracy metrics, throughput dashboards

services.name: Event Grid
services.purpose: Blob-triggered processing — new document upload events to processing pipeline

optimization_tips: Use Document Intelligence prebuilt models first — custom models cost more and need training data
Batch documents for processing during off-peak hours to leverage lower compute costs
Use GPT-4o-mini for simple entity extraction, GPT-4o only for complex classification
Implement blob lifecycle management to tier old documents to Cool/Archive storage
Use Event Grid instead of polling for new documents — eliminates unnecessary compute
Cache extraction results in Cosmos DB to avoid re-processing unchanged documents
